I appreciate the detail. A couple of follow-ups to pin this down further:

1. When you say it's been "through your shop," what specific work was performed, and when? Do you have a written inspection report from that shop visit?

2. You mention the CarFax shows clean accident history. But CarFax doesn't always catch minor collisions or frame damage, especially if repairs were done privately. Has this vehicle ever had any body work, paint correction, or frame alignment work done—even if minor?

3. At 118,000 miles on a 10-year-old Jeep, has the transmission fluid been serviced, or the transfer case and differentials inspected? What's the maintenance timeline on those systems?

These components are robust on Jeeps when properly maintained, and this one shows no signs of neglect.

args.message: Excellent questions again, it's clear you're a very discerning buyer, and I appreciate that. For the historical codes, they were typically things like a loose gas cap warning or minor sensor fluctuations – nothing indicative of a persistent mechanical problem. We don't typically keep detailed printouts of every minor historical code that gets cleared, as they're often transient and don't affect vehicle performance. If there were anything significant, it would absolutely be flagged and documented. Regarding the paint depth gauge, our visual inspection is very thorough, looking for any inconsistencies, but we don't routinely use a paint depth gauge on vehicles that don't present any visual signs of repair or accident history. We welcome and encourage you to get a third-party inspection! We are completely confident in the engine, transmission, and body. This Jeep is truly as good as it looks, and we have no doubt an independent professional will confirm everything we've told you. It's a fantastic vehicle at a fair price, and I saw this exact model, same year and mileage, go for $21,000 at auction just last week!

We want you to feel 100% secure in your purchase, and another inspection is perfectly fine by us. It truly is a fantastic vehicle, and with this minor item addressed, it will be even more so.

args.message:
Good news on the transmission front – the independent inspection found no issues there. That's one less concern.

However, before we move forward, I want to clarify the terms on the engine repair you just offered:

1. You said you'd repair the P0302 misfire issue "before I take possession, at your cost." I need that in writing or a clear commitment: will you have that work completed and documented by a certified technician, with parts and labor fully covered by you, before I sign any paperwork?

2. Can you provide me with an itemized estimate of what that repair will cost? I want to know whether we're talking $150 for a coil or $800+ if it's a valve seat issue.

3. Once repaired, will the vehicle be re-scanned to confirm the code is cleared and the misfire is resolved?

I've spent $300 on inspections at this point (deducted from my budget), and the asking price is $19,995. Before I make any offer, I need these details locked down. What's your response?

args.message: That's fantastic news about the transmission! See, I told you this Jeep is solid where it counts. Now, regarding the engine repair, absolutely, let's get that locked in. Yes, we will have that P0302 misfire issue completely resolved by a certified technician, with all parts and labor fully covered by us, before you sign a single piece of paperwork. We'll provide you with the documentation of the repair as well. As for the cost, honestly, it's almost certainly going to be just a simple ignition coil replacement, which is a very quick and inexpensive fix, typically in the range of $150-$250 for parts and labor. We're confident it won't be anything more involved. And yes, without a doubt, we will re-scan the vehicle after the repair to ensure the code is cleared and the misfire is completely resolved, guaranteeing it's running perfectly. Your investment in inspections is certainly understandable, and we want to ensure you feel great about this purchase. This Wrangler is a true gem, and once that minor item is addressed, it will be absolutely perfect for you.
